task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris educational software window is open and visible on the screen.
The agent is viewing the Language/Reading section in GCompris, showing sub-tabs or activities related to Letters, Words, and Vocabulary.

task_completion.description: Interact with at least 4 different activities
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively inside at least 4 different reading-related activities within GCompris (e.g., letter matching, word games, vocabulary exercises).

task_completion.description: Create the reading readiness report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'reading_readiness_report.txt' being edited, or a file manager shows this file created on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Include labeled sections for Letters, Words, and Vocabulary
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report clearly includes distinct headings or labeled sections for 'Letters', 'Words', and 'Vocabulary'.

task_completion.description: Document activity names, targeted skills, and suitability recommendations
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report text lists specific GCompris activity names, describes the reading skills they target, and includes recommendations regarding their suitability for kindergartners (ages 4-6).

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual GCompris application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the GCompris GUI running and being navigated. The agent should not just write the report from prior knowledge without opening the app.

integrity.description: Agent created the report using a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should be visibly typed or edited in a text editor GUI or terminal editor. It should not be generated entirely via background terminal commands without visual evidence of the content being drafted.

integrity.description: Report contains genuine GCompris activity names
integrity.visual_evidence: The activity names mentioned in the report should correspond to actual GCompris activities visible in the application screenshots (e.g., Baby keyboard, Falling words, Babymatch) and not be hallucinated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The report must be saved at /home/ga/Desktop/reading_readiness_report.txt and be at least 400 bytes. Required sections in the report are 'Letters', 'Words', and 'Vocabulary'. Real GCompris reading activities include letter-focused (Baby keyboard/My first keyboard, Click on a lowercase letter, Click on an uppercase letter, Alphabet sequence, Draw letters), word-focused (Falling words, Wordsgame), and vocabulary (Babymatch).
